questions.question: To every square matrix A, we can associate a number called the:
questions.explanation: To every square matrix A = [a_ij] of order n, we can associate a number (real or complex) called determinant of the square matrix A.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A system of linear equations has a unique solution if the determinant of its coefficient matrix is:
questions.explanation: The system has a unique solution if a1b2 - a2b1 ≠ 0, which is the determinant of the associated matrix.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If A is a matrix of order 1, say A = [a], then det(A) is equal to:
questions.explanation: Determinant of a matrix of order one is the element itself.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Only ________ matrices have determinants.
questions.explanation: Only square matrices have determinants.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The determinant of the 2x2 matrix [[a, b], [c, d]] is given by:
questions.explanation: The determinant of a 2x2 matrix is the product of diagonal elements minus the product of off-diagonal elements.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Expansion of a determinant of order 3 can be done along:
questions.explanation: Determinant of a matrix of order three can be expanded along any row or any column.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: For easier calculations, we should expand the determinant along the row or column which contains maximum number of:
questions.explanation: Expanding along a row or column with more zeros simplifies the summation.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If A and B are square matrices of order n, then |AB| is equal to:
questions.explanation: A fundamental property is that the determinant of a product is the product of determinants.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If A is a square matrix of order n, then |kA| is equal to:
questions.explanation: Multiplying a matrix by a scalar k results in each row being multiplied by k. Extracting k from n rows gives k^n.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The area of a triangle with vertices (x1, y1), (x2, y2), (x3, y3) using determinants is:
questions.explanation: Area is half the absolute value of the determinant formed by coordinates and a column of ones.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If the area of a triangle formed by three points is zero, then the points are:
questions.explanation: If points are collinear, they do not form a triangle, so the area (and the determinant) is zero.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The determinant of a square matrix obtained by interchanging its rows and columns (transpose) is:
questions.explanation: The determinant remains unchanged if rows and columns are interchanged.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If any two rows (or columns) of a determinant are interchanged, then the sign of the determinant:
questions.explanation: Interchanging any two rows or columns changes the sign of the determinant.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If any two rows (or columns) of a determinant are identical, then the value of the determinant is:
questions.explanation: If rows/columns are identical, the determinant value is zero.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The minor of an element a_ij is the determinant obtained by deleting its:
questions.explanation: Minor M_ij is the determinant of the submatrix after deleting the row and column of that element.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The cofactor A_ij of an element a_ij is defined as:
questions.explanation: Cofactor is the minor with a specific sign determined by the position (sum of row and column index).
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If elements of a row are multiplied with cofactors of any other row, then their sum is:
questions.explanation: The sum of products of elements of one row with cofactors of another row is zero.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The adjoint of a square matrix A is the ________ of the matrix of cofactors.
questions.explanation: Adjoint of A is defined as the transpose of the matrix [A_ij].
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A square matrix A is said to be singular if:
questions.explanation: A square matrix is singular if its determinant is zero.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A square matrix A is invertible if and only if A is a:
questions.explanation: Matrix A is invertible if and only if it is non-singular, meaning |A| ≠ 0.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The formula for the inverse of a matrix A is:
questions.explanation: A inverse is calculated by dividing the adjoint of A by its determinant.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A system of equations AX = B is called inconsistent if:
questions.explanation: A system is inconsistent if no solution exists.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If |A| ≠ 0, the unique solution for AX = B is given by:
questions.explanation: Multiplying both sides by A inverse yields X = A⁻¹B.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Which Japanese mathematician showed ideas of determinants in 1683?
questions.explanation: Seki Kowa showed ideas of determinants in his work 'Kai Fukudai no Ho'.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Who was the first to recognize determinants as independent functions and is considered the formal founder?
questions.explanation: Vandermonde may be called the formal founder of the theory of determinants.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Who gave the general method of expanding a determinant in terms of complementary minors in 1772?
questions.explanation: Laplace gave this general method in 1772.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: In 1801, who used determinants in the theory of numbers?
questions.explanation: Gauss used determinants in his theory of numbers in 1801.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Who used the word 'determinant' in its present sense in 1812?
questions.explanation: Cauchy used the word determinant in its present sense and proved the multiplication theorem.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Who was the greatest contributor after whom the word determinant received its final acceptance?
questions.explanation: Carl Gustav Jacob Jacobi was the greatest contributor to the theory.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If elements of a row are written as sum of two terms, the determinant can be expressed as:
questions.explanation: If some or all elements of a row/column are expressed as sum of two terms, then the determinant can be expressed as sum of two determinants.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Area is always a ________ quantity, so we take the absolute value of the determinant.
questions.explanation: Since area is always positive, we use the absolute value of the determinant.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The determinant of a matrix with a row of all zeros is:
questions.explanation: Expansion along the row of zeros results in zero.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: A diagonal matrix has a determinant equal to the product of its:
questions.explanation: For diagonal matrices, the off-diagonal elements are zero, leaving only the product of diagonals in expansion.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The determinant of an identity matrix of any order is:
questions.explanation: The product of diagonal elements (all 1) is 1.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The notation for the determinant of matrix A can be:
questions.explanation: The standard notations include det A, |A|, and Δ.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If elements of any row are multiplied by a constant k, the value of the determinant:
questions.explanation: Multiplying a row by k multiplies the whole determinant by k.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Is the symbol |A| for determinant the same as the 'absolute value' of A?
options: No, for matrices it means determinant
questions.explanation: For matrix A, |A| is read as determinant of A, not modulus.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: In the system AX = B, if B is a zero matrix, the system is called:
questions.explanation: If B = 0, the system of linear equations is homogeneous.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Minor M_ij is defined for which elements?
options: Every element a_ij of the determinant
questions.explanation: A minor is associated with every element in a square matrix.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The determinant of a skew-symmetric matrix of odd order is always:
questions.explanation: A skew-symmetric matrix of odd order always has a zero determinant.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Jacques - Philippe - Marie Binet contributed to which theorem in 1812?
questions.explanation: Binet stated the theorem relating to the determinant of the product of two matrices.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If each element of a row is zero, the value of the determinant is:
questions.explanation: Expansion along that row gives zero.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The adjoint of a diagonal matrix is a ________ matrix.
questions.explanation: Cofactors of off-diagonal elements in a diagonal matrix are zero, keeping it diagonal.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Cofactor of an element at (1, 2) is:
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The determinant value represents a ________ associated with a matrix.
questions.explanation: The determinant associates a single unique number with a square matrix.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: If each element of a row is added to equimultiples of another row, the determinant:
questions.explanation: Row operations of the type Ri -> Ri + kRj do not change the determinant value.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: What is A adj A equal to for a matrix of order n?
questions.explanation: It is a standard theorem that A(adj A) = (adj A)A = |A|I.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Seki Kowa used determinants for eliminating quantities from ________ equations.
questions.explanation: Seki Kowa used it for eliminating a quantity from two equations.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: For a system of equations, if |A| = 0 and (adj A)B ≠ 0, then the system is:
questions.explanation: This condition implies no solution exists for the system AX = B.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.question: Determinants are commonly used to find the ________ of a triangle.
questions.explanation: A primary application of determinants is calculating the area of a triangle given its vertices.
questions.subject: Mathematics
